page: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a virtual machine instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is configured with an x86_64 architecture AMD EPYC-Turin processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The instance features 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation model, meaning threads are not dedicated. It includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, and no GPU accelerators are included.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. In single-core tests, the instance achieves top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core CPU tests, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression. However, under multi-core workloads, performance scales to average levels for stress-ng multi-core, bzip3 decompression, and PassMark CPU scores. Memory performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for the PassMark memory score, while read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks remain in the average tier.

The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff, as multi-core scaling is limited to average tiers despite the high-performing single-core capabilities of the AMD EPYC-Turin architecture. This resource density makes the instance a cost-efficient option for workloads that require substantial memory capacity and strong single-threaded performance without needing sustained, dedicated multi-core execution. It is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, redis caching, and database operations that fit within its network storage constraints.
description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a General Purpose instance in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores under a shared CPU allocation model, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM and 1 GB of network-attached local storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng and compression tests, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth align with average tiers. The PassMark memory score is strong, placing in the top 25%. Due to its shared CPU model, the instance offers a cost-efficient balance of high memory capacity and strong single-threaded capabilities, though it faces multi-core scaling tradeoffs. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a VX1 Cloud Compute instance with 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 64.0 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, delivering top-tier single-core and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64.0 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A shared-CPU instance offering a balanced memory-to-core ratio, top-tier single-threaded performance, and cost-efficient general-purpose compute capabilities.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and compression speeds
1 GB network-attached local storage with no GPU acceleration
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of PassMark benchmarks
Suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database operations
